content.problemStatement:
When you access a Flask route for the first time, you notice that the server logs two requests.
What is the most likely reason for this?



You can clearly see in the running terminal that there are 2 requests sent to the webpage:


- 1st - HEAD request: This request retrieves only the headers (metadata) of the response without downloading the entire page. Browsers do this to check things like cache validation or page existence.

- 2nd - GET request: This is the actual request that loads the page content.

Since Flask logs both requests, it may appear that your route is accessed twice.
If your route includes a counter, it will increment once for the HEAD request and again for the GET request, leading to an incorrect count.

This error is caused only because you are using SEED-IT's Lab environment, which loads the particular webpage that should be shown to you, and then loads the content of the browser.
If you run this code locally on your machine, it should work fine.
